description: Seed patterns for the audit/agentic-ai family. Hits target LLM client setup, tool / function-calling surfaces, system prompt construction, and untrusted content reaching the model. The skill (ToB agentic-actions-auditor) verifies that tool-calls produced by the model are authorised, that user-supplied content cannot pivot the agent into unauthorised actions, and that the system prompt is not silently overridden.
notes: An agentic surface is any code path where an LLM's output becomes an action: shell command, API call, file write, DB mutation. The risk class is prompt injection -> arbitrary tool invocation -> persistence / exfiltration.
Patterns here are intentionally broad — any modern app that imports openai / anthropic SDKs is a candidate. The downstream skill must look at the surrounding code, not just the matched line.
Absence of guardrails (no allow-list of tools, no per-tool capability check, no human-in-the-loop on destructive actions) is the typical finding.

javascript_typescript.description: LLM client instantiation / SDK call site
javascript_typescript.false_positive_notes: Stable; every agentic surface starts from one of these.

javascript_typescript.description: Tool / function-calling schema declared on a request
javascript_typescript.false_positive_notes: When this is present, the model can drive actions; check the dispatcher.

javascript_typescript.description: Server-side dispatch of a model-emitted tool/function call
javascript_typescript.false_positive_notes: This is the critical gate — verify allow-list, arg validation, and capability checks here.
Check whether user input can be concatenated into the system message.
High level marker; the downstream skill should follow imports.

python.description: LLM SDK import / client call site
Tool / function-calling schema declared on a request
python.false_positive_notes: Verify the dispatcher validates args + names.

python.description: Server-side dispatch of a model-emitted tool call
python.false_positive_notes: Critical gate; verify allow-list and capability checks.
Check whether user input can be interpolated.
Drives the question 'what actions can this agent take without human review?'
